important.title: Cross-wave contradiction with T-S2-2 (already flagged by dispatcher)
important.why: S2-docs-memory.tasks.md § T-S2-2 plans the inverse renumbering: keep `ADR-050-accept-langfuse-v3-eol.md` AT 050, and rename `ADR-050-oss-guardrail-providers-ready.md` → ADR-051 and `ADR-050-user-suspend-softdelete.md` → ADR-052. T1.1 just took 052 for langfuse, so if S2 executes verbatim it will re-collide on ADR-052 and overwrite/conflict. Dispatcher has explicitly recorded this in tracking.md L101 with `⚠️ COLLISION CROSS-WAVE` and a `À résoudre USER avant S2` directive. NOT a T1.1 scope violation — T1.1 applied its spec verbatim. Flagged here for traceability only.
important.fix: USER decision needed before S2 dispatch: either (a) reconfigure T-S2-2 so user-suspend → ADR-051 and oss-guardrail → ADR-053, or (b) rewrite T-S2-2 to acknowledge ADR-052 is now taken and pick fresh numbers. No T1.1 rework required.

niceToHave.title: Pre-existing ADR-050 references in unrelated files (out of T1.1 scope, informational)
niceToHave.why: Grep `ADR-050 ` (with trailing space) still returns 4 hits in 2 files. These reference `ADR-050` either prospectively (AI_SAFETY.md L44 'until ADR-050 extends the i18n surface', L158 'first new provider') or as commentary about the triplicate-050 collision itself (T1.4.md). NONE of these references point to the langfuse ADR; they reference the OTHER two ADR-050 files (oss-guardrail, user-suspend) or a hypothetical future ADR-050. Out of T1.1 scope — T1.1 only resolves the langfuse collision.
niceToHave.fix: No action for T1.1. These references should be revisited if T-S2-2 reshuffles the remaining ADR-050 numbering.

niceToHave.title: MASTER.md edits beyond P1-7 line (P0-7, P1-1, P2-6, P2-7) belong to sibling tasks
niceToHave.why: The green-diff snapshot at /tmp/audit-s1-T1.1-green.diff captures MASTER.md hunks on P0-7, P1-1, P2-6, P2-7 in addition to the T1.1 P1-7 hunk on L71. Per the review brief, the worktree is cross-task contaminated (T1.3 / T1.5 / T1.8). The P1-7 hunk on L71 is the ONLY T1.1-owned edit — the other 4 hunks are sibling-task ownership (T1.5 sentry-scrubber P1-1, T1.8 audit-truth reclassifications for P0-7/P2-6/P2-7). NOT a T1.1 scope violation; flagged so the commit-shape step isolates the right hunks.
niceToHave.fix: When committing T1.1, stage ONLY: `git add docs/adr/ADR-052-accept-langfuse-v3-eol.md` (rename target) + `git rm docs/adr/ADR-050-accept-langfuse-v3-eol.md` (rename source — already detected as R by git) + the specific MASTER.md L71 hunk + the OVERNIGHT_P1_REPORT.md L27 hunk. Use `git add -p` or partial-stage to exclude the L54/L65/L93/L94 hunks (those belong to T1.5/T1.8 commits).

verifications.scopeBoundary: PASS for T1.1-owned diff. `git status --short docs/adr/` confirms `RM docs/adr/ADR-050-accept-langfuse-v3-eol.md -> docs/adr/ADR-052-accept-langfuse-v3-eol.md` (rename tracked by git mv). The 2 other ADR-050 files (`ADR-050-oss-guardrail-providers-ready.md`, `ADR-050-user-suspend-softdelete.md`) are UNTOUCHED (no entry in `git status`). MASTER.md and OVERNIGHT_P1_REPORT.md show P1-7 hunks matching spec exactly. Cross-task contamination on MASTER.md (P0-7/P1-1/P2-6/P2-7 hunks) flagged as N2 — out of T1.1 ownership.
verifications.antiHallucination: PASS. Verbatim verifications: (1) `docs/adr/ADR-052-accept-langfuse-v3-eol.md:1` = `# ADR-052 — Accept langfuse v3 EOL until 2026-09-01` (matches spec § T1.1.a); (2) `:3` = `> **Renumbered**: 2026-05-16 from ADR-050 (collision resolution, audit-360 T1.1)` (matches spec § T1.1.a optional bullet, IMPLEMENTED not skipped); (3) `docs/audit-2026-05-12/MASTER.md:71` = `| P1-7 ✅ RESOLVED 2026-05-16 | \`langfuse@v3\` deprecated, aucun patch sécurité futur — resolved 2026-05-16 : ADR-052 accept-EOL daté 2026-05-13, sunset 2026-09-01, T1.1 closed | 08 | ...` (matches spec § T1.1.b verbatim); (4) `docs/audit-2026-05-12/OVERNIGHT_P1_REPORT.md:27` = `| P1-7 | ✅ ADR-052 accepts langfuse v3 EOL until 2026-09-01; tilde-pinned (renamed from ADR-050 on 2026-05-16, collision resolution T1.1) | \`96526b03\` | ...` (ADR-050 → ADR-052 correctly applied); (5) `grep -rln 'ADR-050-accept-langfuse' docs/` = 0 hits (residual path-form references fully cleaned); (6) ADR-052 file is 98 lines vs original 96 = +2 lines = blockquote renumber note + blank line ONLY (similarity index 98% per diff metadata); (7) `wc -l /tmp/audit-s1-T1.1-green.diff` = 71 lines total = small surgical diff.

verifications.honestyContractUFR013: PASS. Renumber note in blockquote on L3 explicitly preserves audit trail (`Renumbered: 2026-05-16 from ADR-050 (collision resolution, audit-360 T1.1)`) — no silent renumber, traceability intact. ADR body (Status/Date/Owner/Sunset/Trigger/Context/Decision/Consequences/Alternatives/References) unchanged byte-for-byte — no rewrite of original 2026-05-13 audit-validated decision. MASTER.md P1-7 flag honestly reports resolution mechanism (`ADR-052 accept-EOL daté 2026-05-13`, not falsely 2026-05-16). OVERNIGHT_P1_REPORT.md preserves original commit hash `96526b03` and notes the rename inline — no hash rewrite, no falsified history.
verifications.kissAssessment: PASS — minimal viable diff. 3 files touched (1 rename + 2 reflag), 71 lines of diff total, +3 net lines added (2 header + 1 blockquote + reflag deltas). No new files created, no doc duplication, no over-engineering. The optional renumber blockquote was IMPLEMENTED (not skipped) — a small quality upgrade beyond the strict AC minimum, justified by UFR-013 traceability.

summary: T1.1 ships a clean, minimal, fully-spec-compliant ADR renumbering (ADR-050-accept-langfuse-v3-eol → ADR-052) that resolves the langfuse-specific 050 collision while preserving the 2026-05-13 audit-validated decision byte-for-byte, plus the 2 sibling reflag updates in MASTER.md L71 and OVERNIGHT_P1_REPORT.md L27. The renumber-traceability blockquote (optional per spec) was implemented — a small UFR-013 quality upgrade. Approved. One important non-blocker finding flags the cross-wave contradiction with S2 task T-S2-2 (which plans the inverse renumbering scheme); this is already documented by the dispatcher in tracking.md L101 as `À résoudre USER avant S2` and does NOT impair T1.1 — it requires a user decision before S2 dispatch.
